description: Unit: resolveDeploymentCapabilities maps 'appliance' -> { provisioner: 'direct', trustForwardedHost: true } and hosted/unset/unknown -> { provisioner: 'temporal', trustForwardedHost: false }.
Unit: the provisioner factory returns the direct driver when caps.portalDomain.provisioner is 'direct' and the temporal driver otherwise.

DB integration (happy path): in direct mode, requestPortalDomainRegistrationAction upserts a portal_domains row with status 'active' and proxy verificationDetails, and the Temporal workflow client is never invoked.
DB integration (destructive): disablePortalDomainAction in direct mode deletes the portal_domains row and cascades away its portal_domain_session_otts rows.
Unit/guard: directProvisioner rejects a domain equal to the NEXTAUTH_URL host and equal to canonicalHost with a clear error, and does not write a row.
Regression (cloud path): in hosted mode, register still upserts status 'pending_dns' and enqueues the portal-domain workflow with the correct trigger (behavior-preserving extraction).
Unit: resolveRequestHost returns the Host hostname by default, honors X-Forwarded-Host only when trustForwardedHost is set, and the resolved host drives vanity-vs-canonical detection.

E2E (critical journey): on an appliance-mode instance with an active vanity domain, a request with Host: portal.acme.com + X-Forwarded-Proto: https drives middleware redirect -> OTT issue -> domain-session consume -> session cookie set on the vanity host.
UI: in direct mode the Settings card renders the BYO-proxy contract checklist (not CNAME-to-canonical), links to the proxy doc, and shows the active/disabled badge; refresh present, retry absent.
Integration (slice 2): an active domain with no recorded last-seen surfaces the 'never seen on its Host' warning, and recording a last-seen for that Host clears it.
Unit (slice 2): middleware emits the Host-rewrite log-warn when X-Forwarded-Host names an active domain but Host does not, and stays silent in the healthy (Host preserved) case.
